affected_groups: Government ministries and tankerület (school district) administrators
Local schools and communities whose fate depends on how discretion is exercised
Parents and pupils seeking predictable, appealable decisions about school continuity
content.decision_question: Administrative flexibility versus rule-bound predictability: the value tension is between discretionary governance that can adapt to local circumstances and binding standards that guarantee consistent, appealable treatment.
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and largely unknown: whether non-binding thresholds actually reduce ad hoc inconsistency in practice, versus binding rules, has not been demonstrated in this record; it rests on an assumption about administrator behaviour that could go either way and would require monitoring data on actual decisions across tankerület to test.
content.tension: Government administrators favor keeping the registry and thresholds explicitly non-binding and advisory, since this preserves ministerial and tankerület discretionary flexibility rather than constraining it with binding rules.
content.title: Decision tension A22: S5
value_poles: rule-bound predictability: the value tension is between discretionary governance that can adapt to local circumstances and binding standards that guarantee consistent, appealable treatment.
